results.title: Memoir of a Snail – Grace's hoard
results.brief_description:
Grace goes to live in Canberra with a well-meaning couple whose interests include self-help books, laughing clubs and leaving keys in bowls. To deal with her loneliness, Grace begins hoarding ornamental snails, guinea pigs and romance novels while indulging in Chiko Rolls. It’s to the garden snail Sylvia that Grace tells her story and where Memoir of a Snail gets its name. Over 3000 components were handmade to create Grace’s hoard. The hoarding aspect of the film is inspired by filmmaker Adam Elliot’s experience helping his mother downsize her belongings. He then studied hoarding and found that it often results from trauma and loss, such as Grace experiences in the film.

Memoir of a Snail opens in cinemas October 17, 2024.

constellations_primary.name: Hoarding
constellations_primary.description: In Memoir of a Snail Grace Pudel's collection of guinea pigs and ornamental snails gets out of hand. We all have emotional connection to the objects we collect, but why do we decide to carry these items throughout our lives, and when does a collection become a hoard?

results.title: Radio day
results.brief_description: A promotional exercise that shows a day in the life of radio station 3XY in Melbourne. Although most of the activities carried out in a commercial radio station are depicted, there is no attempt to explain why and as such this production does little to demystify the process. Suitable for middle and upper secondary levels.

results.title: Gumboots Australia for DOS
results.material_description: Floppy disk videogame. One 3 1/2 inch floppy disk and manual in cardboard box
results.brief_description: Explore Australia and help solve a plethora of mysteries in Gumboots Australia. In this this adventure and educational game, the player is tasked with solving cases as an investigator for the Gumboots Enquiry Agency, a job that will take the player all over the country. The player will get to visit locations as varied as Kakadu to Coober Pedy in their hunt for the next clue. Getting there is its own challenge, as different modes of transportation can cost time and money. Random events along the way aid or hinder the player’s quest to solve the case.

results.title: Frog Detective 3: Corruption at Cowboy County
results.brief_description:
The third and final game in the Frog Detective trilogy finds mystery in Cowboy County, where Frog Detective joins the ‘number-one detective’, Lobster Cop, to solve the case of some stolen hats.
Worm Club combines the series’ usual detective work with the sights and sounds of the Western, as players interview and help a new cast of absurd townsfolk and outlaws. Cowboy County is bigger than the earlier games’ settings, with “vast and rough” terrain to cross. What began as a gag became one of the game’s key features: a scooter system developed by Tom Bowker, turning movement itself into part of the joke.

constellations_primary.name: Classical, comedies and cowboys
constellations_primary.description: This Constellation follows the use of classical music through videogame, TV shows and cinema – often with cowboys.

results.title: Frog Detective 1: The Haunted Island
results.brief_description:
In Frog Detective 1: The Haunted Island, the stereotype of a hard-boiled detective becomes a friendly frog investigating a ghostly noise. Developed by Grace Bruxner while she was still studying game design, this first game in the Frog Detective trilogy turns detective fiction into a silly, kind-hearted adventure.
It borrows from screen mysteries like Agatha Christie’s Poirot (1989), using familiar music, camera angles and episodic structure. But the mystery is deliberately low-stakes, and the detective is polite, enthusiastic and apologetic when suspicious. Through comic dialogue and absurd interviews, Bruxner finds humour in making noir softer, stranger and much more charming.

constellations_other.name: Animal hijinks
constellations_other.description: As long as stories have been told on screen, audiences have loved films, TV shows, memes and videogames about animals being naughty. This constellation shares some key examples of animal comedy throughout the ages.

results.title: Nest of Tens
results.brief_description: ‘Nest of tens’ is comprised of four alternating stories which reveal mundane yet personal methods of control. These systems are derived from intuitive sources. Children and a retarded adult operate control panels made out of paper, lists, monsters and their own bodies. ‘A young boy, home alone, performing a bizarre ritual with a baby; an uneasy, aborted sexual flirtation between a teenage babysitter and an older man; an airport lounge encounter between a businesswoman (played by July) and a young girl. Linked by a lecturer enumerating phobias in a quasi-academic seminar, these three perverse, unnerving scenarios involving children and adults provide authentic glimpses into the queasy strangeness that lies behind the everyday’. New York Video Festival, 2000.

results.title: Loop
results.creator_credit: R. Barrington Scott and Southern Films
results.brief_description: A documentary about Melbourne’s Underground Rail Loop, its effect on the entire metropolitan railway system, and on the future of public transport in Melbourne. Shows the route the loop tracks and tunnels will follow around the city, the sites for the three new underground stations to supplement the present two stations, how passengers will travel from suburban stations when the loop is operating, how the loop is being constructed, and the benefits for train travellers of having five city stations close to shops, theatres and offices. Photographed by Volk Mol, sound by Don Boardman, edited by Geoff Wright, narrated by Michael Pate. Produced for the Melbourne Underground Rail Loop Authority.

results.title: A Search for anti-worlds
results.creator_credit: Philip Dauber, Berkeley Film Associates and Pyramid Films
results.brief_description: Documents the course of physics research experiments at the Lawrence Berkeley Laboratory of the University of California, which seeks to determine the existence of large amounts of antimatter in the universe.
